en_title: Praying With The Sound Of Breath
en_body:
The-Thirtieth-Of-April...And so thirty-two years have gone...What can be said inside this strangled remembrance? Though knowing still that the present moment is an imprint of the transformation of the past into the future, sometimes seeing clearly it carries many surprises of Indeterminacy: the river of life is forever flowing away, the clouds of heaven alter their forms and exist only once! But memory is not one-dimensional: it compresses time inside remembrance. And the price of such meditations is that backward-gazing measure of the present: and the price is well worth paying: for human beings must continually redefine themselves in the present by the past, as a house is built upon its foundation.
 Like the story of the little boy who used his finger to stop the hole in the dike in a Dutch fairy tale, how can one express the tide of thought with all the emotions borne through thirty-two years? I can only sorrowfully murmur Thanh Tâm Tuyền's lines and hear burning tears sobbing deep, so very deep, from the bottom of myself...
 "...Seeing one away, I see no one across a river
 Why do I hear the sound of waves inside my heart
 The evening shadow is neither crimson nor wan
 Why are my inmost eyes filled with sunset..."
 O Dawn!...the same sun pouring forth the dazzling light of sunset: I go seeking you, longing for us to wait for one another! Do not lose each other only because we remain hidden beyond the arching road of the horizon and imagine we cannot see each other!
note: April 30 marks the 1975 fall of Saigon, and the quoted stanza is Thanh Tâm Tuyền's modern reimagining of an ancient farewell poem.
